title: Long service leave  | Employment New Zealand
content:
Employees can negotiate long-service leave and long-service leave benefits with their employer.
Long-service leave Long service leave A period of additional leave that an employer can choose to give employees who have completed an extended period of service. is a period of additional leave granted to employees Employee Any person, whatever their age, who is employed by an employer to do any work for hire or reward under a ‘contract of service’ (commonly called an ‘employment agreement’). who have completed an extended period of service with an employer. Other benefits may also be offered for long service.
A period of additional leave that an employer can choose to give employees who have completed an extended period of service.
Any person, whatever their age, who is employed by an employer to do any work for hire or reward under a ‘contract of service’ (commonly called an ‘employment agreement’).
Long-service leave and benefits are not legal requirements but can be negotiated between an employer and employee as additional entitlements under an employment agreement Employment agreement A written document setting out the terms and conditions of employment agreed by the employer and employee (also known as a ‘contract of service’). It can include other contractual documents and agreements made by the employer and employee. Every employee must have a written employment agreement. , or workplace policy Workplace policy Workplace policies and procedures explain the rules and expectations in the workplace. . The length of time required for an employee to qualify for long-service leave will depend on what they have agreed to with their employer.
Employment agreement
A written document setting out the terms and conditions of employment agreed by the employer and employee (also known as a ‘contract of service’). It can include other contractual documents and agreements made by the employer and employee. Every employee must have a written employment agreement.
Workplace policies and procedures explain the rules and expectations in the workplace.
